jokes.text: I just overheard three women with smokers' coughs talking about 'Sex in the City'. The singles have arrived!
jokes.setup: Moira dramatically announces the arrival of singles
jokes.punchline: She identifies them by their smokers' coughs and SATC discussion
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira pretension - she notices superficial stereotypes and announces them with theatrical flair

jokes.text: I think it would be a tad déclassé for the chair of the event to be wearing a Volunteers t-shirt
jokes.setup: Moira explains why she won't wear the event t-shirt
jokes.punchline: She thinks a volunteer t-shirt is beneath her at a volunteer event
jokes.explanation: Classic Moira pretension - missing the point that she IS a volunteer

jokes.text: I appreciate everything you do, Alexis, but I think it's best I carry the baton over the goal line
jokes.setup: Mother-daughter conflict about event planning
jokes.punchline: Moira mixes sports metaphors while being condescending
jokes.explanation: Moira's tendency to use inappropriate metaphors while being patronizing

jokes.text: The Teenage Rosacea Ball at the St. Regis?
jokes.setup: Reference to their last co-chaired event
jokes.punchline: A formal ball themed around teenage acne at a luxury hotel
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist invention - the juxtaposition of formal elegance with teenage skin problems is wonderfully ridiculous

jokes.text: Um, I just walked past three very raspy-voiced Miranda's outside
jokes.setup: Reporting on the arrival of singles
jokes.punchline: Characterizing them as raspy-voiced Miranda Hobbes types
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that builds on the SATC stereotype with specific character typing

jokes.text: Roland's phone. Hey, Bob. Roland, hang up. Yeah, uh, Johnny's tellin' me I gotta hang up. Yes, they're both here, staring at me. Johnny has that upset look on his face
jokes.setup: Roland takes a personal call during business meeting
jokes.punchline: He narrates the awkward situation to the caller while Johnny gets increasingly frustrated
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy - Roland's obliviousness creates escalating awkwardness

jokes.text: That's the perfect way to describe it, Bob
jokes.setup: Roland continuing the inappropriate phone call
jokes.punchline: He validates Bob's description of Johnny's anger while Johnny stands right there
jokes.explanation: Escalates the cringe by having Roland agree with insults about Johnny

jokes.text: How 'bout a car wash? Or maybe a brow trim for Johnny?
jokes.setup: Roland asks what else Johnny might need
jokes.punchline: Suggests a brow trim as if it's equivalent to business tasks
jokes.explanation: Roland's casual personal insults disguised as helpfulness

jokes.text: You been goin' to the gym, 'cause that looked effortless?
jokes.setup: David struggles with a box then claims it's awkwardly shaped
jokes.punchline: Stevie sarcastically compliments his 'effortless' struggle
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stevie deadpan - she highlights David's vanity while acknowledging his actual effort

jokes.text: It's just a very awkwardly shaped box, but yes I have, and thank you for noticing
jokes.setup: David struggling with a box
jokes.punchline: He deflects the struggle but can't resist acknowledging the gym compliment
jokes.explanation: Perfect David vanity - can't let a compliment pass unacknowledged even while making excuses

jokes.text: The whole situation with Alexis and Ted right now is very awkward and cringe-y. Alexis told him she loved him
jokes.setup: David explaining why he can't deliver the box
jokes.punchline: He describes emotional vulnerability as 'cringe-y' and immediately spills the tea
jokes.explanation: Classic David - emotionally avoidant but can't resist gossip

jokes.text: I just think it'll be less awkward for all of us, especially considering Ted now knows that I know, that he knows, if you maybe took the box
jokes.setup: David trying to convince Stevie to deliver the box
jokes.punchline: Gets tangled in convoluted 'who knows what' logic
jokes.explanation: Good David spiral - overthinking social dynamics into linguistic pretzel

jokes.text: Okay, so... so you just said that to me for the first time, knowing that it would make my day more stressful. That's correct
jokes.setup: Patrick says 'I love you' for first time then admits it will stress David
jokes.punchline: David processes the romantic timing logic, Patrick confirms the contradiction
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character work - Patrick's emotional honesty meets David's need to analyze everything

jokes.text: Because you know that I've never said that to anyone else, aside from my parents twice, and one time at a... Mariah Carey concert
jokes.setup: David explaining why this is stressful
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's only said 'I love you' to parents twice and once to Mariah Carey
jokes.explanation: Perfect David character revelation - the specificity of 'twice' to parents and the Mariah detail is pure gold

jokes.text: You're my Mariah Carey
jokes.setup: David trying to reciprocate Patrick's declaration
jokes.punchline: Uses the Mariah Carey concert as his highest form of emotional expression
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback - David finds his own emotionally authentic way to say it back

jokes.text: David awkwardly carrying the box out while asking Patrick not to look
jokes.setup: David revealed his emotional vulnerability about the box
jokes.punchline: Visual of him awkwardly struggling with it while trying to preserve dignity
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy capper to the emotional scene - David's vanity vs reality

jokes.text: That went well
jokes.setup: The entire awkward 'I love you' exchange and box struggle
jokes.punchline: Patrick's understated assessment of the chaos
jokes.explanation: Perfect button to the scene - Patrick's dry delivery contrasts the emotional chaos

jokes.text: Jocelyn is in labor!
jokes.setup: Moira explaining why she can't get the keys
jokes.punchline: The absurdly dramatic reason that derails the singles event
jokes.explanation: Solid plot complication that creates chaos for perfectionist event planners

jokes.text: Oh! Don't start without me you little frippit. You don't have the media training
jokes.setup: Moira coaching Jocelyn through labor while worried about the event
jokes.punchline: Calls the unborn baby a 'frippit' and questions its media readiness
jokes.explanation: Peak Moira absurdity - treating birth as a media event and inventing 'frippit'

jokes.text: Wait, Jocelyn's driving? Yes, Alexis! She insisted! My nerves are fried!
jokes.setup: The labor emergency situation
jokes.punchline: The woman in labor is driving while Moira panics about her nerves
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist reversal - Moira's comfort is the concern, not the laboring woman driving

jokes.text: Uh-oh. I think that's Roland's phone
jokes.setup: Mysterious ringing phone in the office
jokes.punchline: Realization they have the phone of someone whose wife is in labor
jokes.explanation: Good escalation setup for the Roland phone plot

jokes.text: Roland, it's happening. And then baby. And then, like a whole row of baby emojis, and an explosion
jokes.setup: Reading the increasingly urgent texts from Jocelyn
jokes.punchline: The progression from words to baby emojis to explosion emoji
jokes.explanation: Good modern comedy - the escalation through emoji use perfectly captures labor urgency

jokes.text: I didn't. You're the one who put his phone on silent. Because you didn't know how to
jokes.setup: Johnny trying to assign blame for the phone situation
jokes.punchline: Stevie turns it back on Johnny's tech incompetence
jokes.explanation: Good callback that escalates the blame game with character-specific details

jokes.text: It's always gifts for the dogs, and never for me. Definitely one of my pet peeves
jokes.setup: David delivering dog sweaters for pet adoption
jokes.punchline: Ted makes a 'pet peeves' pun about not getting gifts while the dogs do
jokes.explanation: Solid pun work - 'pet peeves' in context of actual pets and gifts

jokes.text: Um... no, your game is... tight
jokes.setup: Ted saying David's presence has thrown him off his game
jokes.punchline: David's awkward, unconvincing reassurance about Ted's 'tight' game
jokes.explanation: David's social awkwardness in emotional situations - the pause and word choice are perfectly uncomfortable

jokes.text: Yes. No, that is an emotional depth that is very, very out of character for her
jokes.setup: Ted processing that Alexis said 'I love you'
jokes.punchline: David 'comforts' Ted by confirming Alexis showing emotion is abnormal
jokes.explanation: Perfect David - trying to help but actually making it worse by insulting his sister

jokes.text: I broke up with Heather
jokes.setup: David trying to leave after the awkward conversation
jokes.punchline: Ted drops major news just as David is escaping
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic timing - drops the bomb just as David thinks he's escaping

jokes.text: So I should probably stay, then, for a minute?
jokes.setup: Ted's revelation about breaking up with Heather
jokes.punchline: David's reluctant realization he's now stuck in emotional conversation
jokes.explanation: David's resigned acceptance of being trapped in feelings conversation

jokes.text: Hey, singles, I'm Alexis Rose, and I, like all of you, am single
jokes.setup: Alexis opening the singles event with confidence
jokes.punchline: The pause and emphasis on being single hits differently given her recent heartbreak
jokes.explanation: The pause and emphasis reveal the pain underneath her professional presentation

jokes.text: Now, I used to think that my one special lock needed to be like super aggressive, and come from a long line of Adriatic royalty
jokes.setup: Alexis explaining her past dating preferences via lock metaphor
jokes.punchline: Hilariously specific reference to 'Adriatic royalty' as relationship criteria
jokes.explanation: Perfect Alexis character work - the specificity of 'Adriatic royalty' captures her past shallow criteria

jokes.text: or have a beard, and be really into the woods
jokes.setup: Continuing her list of past relationship requirements
jokes.punchline: Obviously describing Mutt, her woodsy ex-boyfriend
jokes.explanation: Good character callback - fans know exactly who she's describing

jokes.text: So instead of actually giving it a real chance, you throw it away because it was too nice
jokes.setup: Alexis's lock metaphor becoming clearly about Ted
jokes.punchline: She admits to throwing away someone for being 'too nice' to a room of strangers
jokes.explanation: Powerful cringe - she's processing her regret publicly through the metaphor

jokes.text: So, are we playing a game, or not?
jokes.setup: Alexis's entire emotional confession disguised as event instructions
jokes.punchline: Confused attendee cuts through the therapy session to ask about the actual event
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe comedy button - audience surrogate breaking through the emotional metaphor maze

jokes.text: Oh, hold on, Gwen, uh, Roland just pulled in, and hammer's about to drop.
jokes.setup: Johnny sees Roland arriving and realizes confrontation is imminent
jokes.punchline: His ominous 'hammer's about to drop' about facing an angry new father
jokes.explanation: Sets up tension but the phrase itself isn't particularly funny or original.

jokes.text: Roland, Jocelyn's in labor. What? Wh-when did she, why didn't she call me? I gotta call her. I need my phone. I can't... Where's my phone? I can't find my phone?
jokes.setup: Roland finally returns, Johnny has to deliver the news
jokes.punchline: Roland's panic escalates to frantically looking for the phone they've had all along
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the phone plot - Roland's panic about the phone he doesn't know they have

jokes.text: Is a little harsh, but he's definitely slippery, I'll give ya that. I'm standing right here, Bob
jokes.setup: Johnny on phone with Bob discussing Roland
jokes.punchline: Johnny agrees Roland is 'slippery' while Roland stands right there
jokes.explanation: Good cringe callback to earlier Bob conversation - Johnny can't resist agreeing

jokes.text: You know how many off-road dirt bike tours I had to take, just to get her outta my head? Shockingly, never been on a dirt bike. Um... But I'm sure like a lot-a lot... A lot of tours, David
jokes.setup: Ted describing his post-breakup Mexico trip
jokes.punchline: David's awkward attempt to relate despite never being on a dirt bike
jokes.explanation: Good David fish-out-of-water moment - trying to relate to outdoor activities he'd never do

jokes.text: I've been burned so many times, I'm basically the human equivalent of the inside of a roasted marshmallow
jokes.setup: David relating to Ted being burned in relationships
jokes.punchline: Hilariously specific metaphor comparing himself to marshmallow interior
jokes.explanation: Brilliant David metaphor - perfectly captures his emotional damage with vivid, specific imagery

jokes.text: That's a dog treat? Looks like a real treat. What am I gonna do now?
jokes.setup: David giving Ted what he thinks is a thank you treat
jokes.punchline: Ted realizes he just ate a dog biscuit and now has to process this
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy beat - the realization and Ted's practical concern about what to do now

jokes.text: I have watched your daughter, over the last couple of years, grow into one of the most amazing people I've ever known. She is a hard worker, she is kind, and she is capable of so much more than people give her credit for
jokes.setup: Moira worried about leaving Alexis in charge
jokes.punchline: The woman in labor gives the most articulate, touching defense of Alexis
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment - unexpected wisdom from Jocelyn while in labor

jokes.text: There she is! Okay, I found it! Third time's a charm! Rolie, you made it! You may step down, ma'am, I thank you for your service
jokes.setup: Roland arrives to find Moira helping Jocelyn through labor
jokes.punchline: He treats Moira like a substitute coach being relieved of duty
jokes.explanation: Good Roland character work - treating birth like a sports substitution

jokes.text: Oh, if the drugs are any good, you will
jokes.setup: Jocelyn saying she'll never forget Moira's help
jokes.punchline: Moira's dark joke about labor drugs causing memory loss
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira dark humor - subverts the sentimental moment with medical reality

jokes.text: Well, David, I gotta hand it to you. We've already sold half of our massage oils. It's like you're an oracle when it comes to the sex lives of lonely people.
jokes.setup: Stevie comments on their Singles Week merchandise success
jokes.punchline: She credits David's expertise in understanding desperate singles' needs
jokes.explanation: Good Stevie observation about David's understanding of loneliness, delivered with her typical dry tone.

jokes.text: I love you
jokes.setup: Stevie asking how it went with Ted
jokes.punchline: David responds with the phrase he's been struggling with all episode
jokes.explanation: Beautiful character growth moment - David finally says it easily, showing his evolution

jokes.text: I know I'll never be able to compete with Mariah
jokes.setup: David's 'I love you' declaration
jokes.punchline: Stevie references the earlier Mariah Carey comparison with perfect deadpan
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stevie callback - she was listening and delivers it with her signature dry wit

jokes.text: No, I am making this perfect moment, perfect.
jokes.setup: David mentions his dream moment would include tea
jokes.punchline: Patrick's determination to literally perfect their romantic moment by getting tea
jokes.explanation: Patrick's earnest commitment to David's specific vision of romance is sweet and in character.

jokes.text: Um, actually you may not be able to tell by the way those two are gazing into each other's eyes, they actual work for rival newspapers
jokes.setup: Apparent romantic success at the singles event
jokes.punchline: The 'romance' is actually competitive journalism
jokes.explanation: Good subversion of romantic expectations - what looks like love is actually work rivalry

jokes.text: Um, actually you may not be able to tell by the way those two are gazing into each other's eyes, they actual work for rival newspapers.
jokes.setup: Alexis points out a successful romantic connection at the event
jokes.punchline: The romantic couple are actually rival journalists who've found love
jokes.explanation: Nice detail that adds to the event's success while commenting on small-town journalism dynamics.

jokes.text: Seems there's a lot more of me in you, than I give you credit for. Lucky me
jokes.setup: Moira complimenting Alexis on the event success
jokes.punchline: Alexis's sarcastic response to being compared to Moira
jokes.explanation: Good family dynamic - Alexis's dry response to Moira's backhanded compliment

jokes.text: Oh, what a handsome guy. He looks just like me. And yet, he's handsome.
jokes.setup: Roland admires his newborn son
jokes.punchline: He notes the baby looks like him but is somehow still handsome
jokes.explanation: Unexpectedly sweet and self-aware from Roland. The logic is perfectly flawed and endearing.

jokes.text: So, we'd like you to say hello to Roland Moira Schitt
jokes.setup: Roland introducing the baby to Moira
jokes.punchline: They named the baby 'Roland Moira Schitt' - a terrible name
jokes.explanation: Great absurd naming choice - 'Roland Moira Schitt' is objectively terrible and hilarious

jokes.text: Wow, that's... the name is really something, isn't it? And an honor I dream not of!
jokes.setup: Hearing the baby's terrible name
jokes.punchline: Moira's diplomatic horror at being honored with such a name
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira diplomacy - she's horrified but tries to be gracious about the naming honor

jokes.text: Look at that, the two Moira's. Take him, John, my hands are shaking
jokes.setup: Roland forcing the baby on reluctant Moira
jokes.punchline: Moira's physical panic at holding the baby she's now namesake to
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy - Moira's terror at baby-holding creates genuine panic
